description: When the exact design does not matter, 'molu' refers to an object used to receive, hold, store, or carry contents. It includes boxes, bags, bottles, pots, and many other forms. 'lopha' (vessel) selects a formed hollow receptacle, especially one holding a substance; Household words distinguish narrower designs.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm' and open into stressed mid-back rounded 'o'. Place the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l', then raise the back of the tongue and keep the lips rounded for 'u'.
usage_notes: The contents can precede the container when a stable compound or context needs them. Capacity and closure may matter; material and safe use can be stated alongside them.

semantic_domains.physical: an object used to hold, store, or carry contents
semantic_domains.spatial: a bounded interior considered in relation to what it contains
